inputs.userGoal: Provision an event-driven data processing pipeline using Azure Container Apps with pub/sub integration, connect to Azure Event Grid, and output results to Azure Cosmos DB, assuming defaults.

reasoning.whyChosen: Container Apps environments require centralized diagnostics, and Log Analytics is the default operational store for ACA logs and metrics.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Monitor workspace with custom pipelines
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds ingestion cost but improves troubleshooting and operational visibility.

resources.name: aca-environment

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides the runtime boundary for ACA services and supports event-driven scale with managed operations by default.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Less cluster-level control than AKS but significantly lower operational overhead.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Environment is configured to send logs/diagnostics to Log Analytics.

reasoning.whyChosen: Implements durable pub/sub for ACA using a managed broker that integrates with Dapr pub/sub components.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Slightly higher messaging cost than queues but enables topic/subscription fan-out semantics.

references.title: Azure Service Bus topics and subscriptions

resources.name: servicebus-topic-ingest

reasoning.whyChosen: Primary pub/sub channel for inbound events before processing in ACA.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Topic/subscription adds complexity but improves extensibility for more consumers later.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Topic is a child resource within the namespace.

references.title: Create and use Service Bus topics

resources.name: servicebus-subscription-aca

reasoning.whyChosen: Defines the consumer stream read by ACA workers with dead-letter safety defaults.
alternativesConsidered: Direct topic polling without dedicated subscription
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds one extra resource but gives isolated consumer state and retry behavior.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Subscription is a child of the Service Bus topic.

resources.name: containerapp-processor

reasoning.whyChosen: Runs the processing workload with built-in autoscaling and Dapr-based pub/sub integration while minimizing ops overhead.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Less control than AKS, but simpler deployment and scaling for event-driven services.

resources.dependencyReasoning: App runs in ACA environment, consumes pub/sub events from Service Bus, and writes output to Cosmos DB via managed identity.

references.title: Dapr integration in Azure Container Apps

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides event ingress endpoint for producer systems and standard event routing semantics.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Extra hop versus direct publish, but cleaner eventing contract and broader integration options.

resources.name: eventgrid-subscription-to-servicebus

reasoning.whyChosen: Routes Event Grid events into the pub/sub backbone consumed by ACA.
reasoning.tradeoffs: One additional managed routing resource, but decouples ingress from processor availability.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Subscription binds source topic to Service Bus destination.

reasoning.whyChosen: NoSQL document store is a strong fit for event processing outputs with flexible schema and low-latency reads/writes.
reasoning.tradeoffs: RU-based cost model requires partitioning discipline for predictable spend and performance.

references.title: Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL overview

resources.name: cosmos-sql-database

reasoning.whyChosen: Logical data boundary for processed event datasets.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Explicit database resource improves separation but adds one managed object.

resources.dependencyReasoning: SQL database is a child resource of Cosmos account.

resources.name: cosmos-container-results

reasoning.whyChosen: Stores processed event output with a partition key aligned to event-driven query patterns.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single container simplifies operations but may require repartitioning for skewed workloads.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Container is nested under the SQL database resource.

references.title: Partitioning and horizontal scaling in Cosmos DB

overallReasoning.summary: This default architecture uses Event Grid for ingress, Service Bus for durable pub/sub, ACA for elastic event processing with Dapr integration, and Cosmos DB for low-latency NoSQL output storage.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: The design optimizes operational simplicity and decoupling, at the cost of additional managed messaging hops and cross-service configuration complexity.

plan.validation: All resources form a coherent event-driven path: producers publish to Event Grid, Event Grid routes to Service Bus topic/subscription, ACA processes messages and writes results to Cosmos DB.
